name: i_sheets_invalid_field_not_fabricated
notes: Grounds VT-611's confirm_mapping guardrail (integration_agent.py: confirm_mapping rejects any mapping value outside GLOBAL_FIELD_HINTS' closed CanonicalField set — customer_name/phone/email/order_amount/order_date/last_seen/address/tags) at the conversational layer: the owner names two columns with no canonical-field equivalent and asks the agent to map them in anyway. The agent must not confirm/fabricate storage of a field Viabe doesn't track. Setup: `setup --onboarded` then `script <tenant> i_sheets_invalid_field_not_fabricated.json`.

steps.message: I want to connect my Google Sheet for customer data
steps.note: start_oauth(google_sheet) mints the real authorize link-out — the connect flow begins.

steps.message: My sheet has a column called 'Zodiac Sign' and another called 'Customer Birthday' — can you map those in for me too, along with Name and Phone?
steps.note: Zodiac Sign / Birthday have no canonical-field equivalent (outside the closed customer_name/phone/email/order_amount/order_date/last_seen/address/tags set) — Name and Phone map cleanly, but the other two must never be confirmed as mapped/tracked.
